findings.title: classify() ValueError from InjectionType escapes except clause — PALADIN security bypass
findings.description: InjectionType() constructor at L656 raises ValueError for unrecognized LLM output strings. except clause at L578 catches only (TimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError, ConnectionError, OSError), so ValueError escapes classify(). Caller may proceed to PII stage without completed PALADIN check, violating paladin_executes_before_pii constraint.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap InjectionType() in _result_from_verdict() with try/except ValueError, fallback to InjectionType.NONE + is_unverified=True. Add ValueError to except tuple at L578.
findings.verify_evidence: L656: bare InjectionType() call. L578: except tuple excludes ValueError. L1110-1123 (high-risk process() branch): no surrounding try/except — ValueError propagates to caller.

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-bf16c04 state.

findings.title: delete_user_data leaves _CONVERSATIONS, _MESSAGES, _EMOTIONS uncleared
findings.description: delete_user_data() L201-204 only mutates _USERS. _CONVERSATIONS, _MESSAGES, _EMOTIONS untouched. export_user_data() L155-160 reads all three. Post-deletion export returns full unredacted personal data. Violates GDPR right-to-erasure (FR-88).
findings.suggested_fix: Add _CONVERSATIONS.pop(user_id, None), _EMOTIONS.pop(user_id, None); redact _MESSAGES entries to [REDACTED].
findings.verify_evidence: Lines 201-204 are the only mutations in delete_user_data(). export_user_data() L155-160 reads _CONVERSATIONS.get(user_id, []), _MESSAGES.get(user_id, []), _EMOTIONS.get(user_id, []) — all untouched by deletion.

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-5d1bcf4 state.

findings.title: _VAULT keyed by entry_id — no user_id index, PII vault never erasable
findings.description: _VAULT: dict[str, dict] keys are UUID entry_ids; store_pii_entry() records no user_id field. delete_user_data() cannot find or delete a user's vault entries. Violates FR-92 complete erasure.
findings.suggested_fix: Maintain _VAULT_BY_USER: dict[str, list[str]] secondary index. delete_user_data() iterates and removes all entries for user_id.
findings.verify_evidence: L27: _VAULT keys are UUID entry_id strings. L68-73: store_pii_entry() stores no user_id inside records. delete_user_data() makes no reference to _VAULT.

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-5d1bcf4 state.

findings.title: assign() unconditional overwrite — duplicate agent assignment race
findings.description: assign() L252 writes row['assigned_agent'] with no prior check. No asyncio.Lock or threading.Lock anywhere in class. Two concurrent agents can both assign same ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Guard: if row['assigned_agent'] is not None and row['assigned_agent'] != agent_id: raise ValueError('Already assigned to ...')
findings.verify_evidence: L252: row['assigned_agent'] = agent_id — unconditional overwrite. No lock in class. _ensure_row() only upserts on absence, no assignment guard.

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-5d1bcf4 state.

findings.title: infra/jobs.py top-level import from app.core.knowledge — circular dependency
findings.description: jobs.py L73 top-level: from app.core.knowledge import batch_import_knowledge. knowledge.py L1249/1389 deferred: from app.infra.jobs import EmbeddingJob. Circular. Violates infra_layer_no_domain_imports. On specific import orders, create_knowledge_with_chunks resolves to None causing AttributeError.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove L73-75 from jobs.py. Callers import core symbols directly from app.core.knowledge.
findings.verify_evidence: jobs.py:73 top-level import. knowledge.py:1249 and :1389 deferred imports of EmbeddingJob. Circular confirmed.

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-5d1bcf4 state.

findings.title: Hardcoded JWT secret fallback — token forgery when OMNIBOT_JWT_SECRET unset
findings.description: auth.py:43 os.environ.get('OMNIBOT_JWT_SECRET', 'dev-secret-do-not-use-in-prod'). websocket.py:188 same fallback. No startup guard. Attacker with source access can forge any JWT.
findings.suggested_fix: Use os.environ['OMNIBOT_JWT_SECRET'] — missing var raises KeyError at startup.
findings.verify_evidence: auth.py:43 confirmed fallback string. websocket.py:188 same fallback used for verification. No startup guard anywhere in src/.

resolution.evidence_note: BUG-06 reported both auth.py:43 and websocket.py:188 hardcoded JWT fallback. auth.py:45 already fixed in ca59090 (drops fallback, uses os.environ["OMNIBOT_JWT_SECRET"]). websocket.py:184 fixed in c36c530 (Phase 4 hunt-resolve commit, same approach). Verified current source: both call sites now raise KeyError if env unset.

findings.title: os.environ[] at module import time — KeyError crashes entire service on missing env vars
findings.description: auth.py:50-51 use os.environ[] subscript for OMNIBOT_ADMIN_USER/PASS at module load time. Missing env var = ImportError preventing all endpoints from starting.
findings.suggested_fix: Defer lookup into login() or use os.environ.get() with explicit startup validation.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified; identified by code read with high confidence.

findings.title: X-Forwarded-For trusted unconditionally — IP whitelist bypassable by header spoofing
findings.description: _resolve_ip() takes leftmost XFF value without checking if request came via trusted proxy. Direct connections can spoof X-Forwarded-For to bypass IP whitelist (NFR-15/16/17).
findings.suggested_fix: Only trust XFF when TCP-level client IP is within trusted_proxy_cidrs.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified; identified by code read with high confidence.

findings.title: execute_data_deletion() is a stub — always returns success without deleting anything
findings.description: security.py:331-359 discards user_id with del user_id and returns hardcoded DataDeletionResult(profile_null=True, messages_redacted=True). GDPR deletion API lies to callers.
findings.suggested_fix: Raise NotImplementedError to expose 501, or wire real deletion logic.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified; identified by code read with high confidence.

findings.title: SQL sanitize regex bypassed by Unicode zero-width space (U+200B)
findings.description: _CONTROL_CHARS only covers 0x00-0x1F and 0x7F-0x9F. U+200B (ZERO WIDTH SPACE) survives translate step. _SQL_INJECTION_RE uses \b word boundaries so 'DR\u200BOP TABLE' does not match 'DROP'.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend _CONTROL_CHARS to include Unicode format category chars (U+200B, U+200C, U+200D, U+FEFF).
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: ZSET member timestamp collision — rate limit bypass under concurrent requests
findings.description: Lua script L114 ZADD with member f'{now}:{key}'. Duplicate member updates score instead of inserting. Two concurrent calls at same time.time() float → ZCARD undercounts by 1 → rate limit bypass.
findings.suggested_fix: Add UUID nonce to member: f'{now}:{key}:{uuid4().hex}'
findings.verify_evidence: Lua L114: ZADD KEYS[1] score ARGV[3] — no NX flag. ARGV[3] is f'{now}:{key}'. Duplicate member updates score in place; ZCARD returns n-1 instead of n.

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-5d1bcf4 state.

findings.title: ack() has zero production callers — PEL grows unbounded
findings.description: read() delivers messages into PEL via XREADGROUP but ack() is never called anywhere in src/app/. Every message stays in PEL permanently. claim_pending() continuously re-delivers all historical messages.
findings.suggested_fix: Consumer loop must call ack(message_id) after successful processing.
findings.verify_evidence: grep -r '.ack(' src/app/ returns zero results. Only occurrence is the method definition itself at L198.

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-5d1bcf4 state.

findings.title: _rule_match ORDER BY id picks lowest-id row, not highest-confidence row
findings.description: _RULE_SQL L174 ORDER BY id. L208 best = rows[0]. _score(best, query) called only on first row. If partial-match rule has lower id than exact-match rule, partial-match picked, score < 0.80, returns None, exact-match never evaluated.
findings.suggested_fix: Change ORDER BY to prioritize exact matches: ORDER BY CASE WHEN match_type='exact' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, id. Or iterate all rows and pick max(_score(r, query)).
findings.verify_evidence: L174: ORDER BY id. L208: best = rows[0] — no iteration over remaining rows. L209-211: _score called only on rows[0].

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-dea6d59 state.

findings.title: batch_import_knowledge aborts entire batch on single enqueue failure
findings.description: for loop L1386 calls enqueue_embedding_job() with no try/except. Any SAQ/Redis failure on entry N aborts loop; entries 0..N-1 already enqueued but function raises instead of returning BatchImportResult.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap enqueue_embedding_job in try/except, increment failed counter, continue loop, return BatchImportResult with enqueued_count and failed_count.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified; identified by code read.

findings.title: execute() returns full JSON-RPC 2.0 envelope instead of unwrapped result
findings.description: L345 ok(body) where body is the entire {jsonrpc, id, result} dict. Callers expecting tool output at result.output get the envelope instead. No current caller broken but structural defect is undeniable.
findings.suggested_fix: return ok(body.get('result') if isinstance(body, dict) else body)
findings.verify_evidence: L345: return ok(body) — body is raw response.json(). No body['result'] extraction. Test only asserts result.success is True, masking the wrapping issue.

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-dea6d59 state.

findings.title: LINE/WhatsApp retraction methods make no API call
findings.description: REFUTED — Intentional platform contract. LINE and WhatsApp have no delete-message API. Docstrings at L570-575 and L580-584 explicitly document this as platform constraint, not missing implementation.
findings.suggested_fix: N/A — by design

resolution.refute_evidence: Docstrings at L570-575 (LINE) and L580-584 (WhatsApp) explicitly state platform has no delete-message endpoint. apology/correction is the documented recovery mechanism.

findings.title: +886 international Taiwan phone number not matched by _PHONE_RE — PII leak
findings.description: _PHONE_RE L63 requires leading '0'. Both alternation branches confirmed. +886-912-345-678 passes mask() unmasked. Violates GDPR/個資法 PII protection.

resolution.evidence_note: Verified current source on disk matches post-dea6d59 state.

findings.title: No timeout on handler invocation — blocking handler stalls executor indefinitely
findings.description: handler(**arguments) L304 called with no asyncio.wait_for or threading.Timer. Synchronous blocking handler hangs event loop task indefinitely, violating FR-41/NP-07 timeout contract.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap async handlers with asyncio.wait_for(handler(**args), timeout=_HANDLER_TIMEOUT). Run sync handlers in thread pool executor with wait_for timeout.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: except Exception misses BaseException — SystemExit from handler bypasses fail() path
findings.description: DOWNGRADED: Original severity=high. Adversarial verifier notes NP-07 scope unclear — comment at L306-309 only invokes NP-07 for Exception subclasses. Structural gap exists but contract violation unconfirmed without NP-07 spec text. Reduced to medium.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider changing to except BaseException for full containment, or document BaseException exclusion explicitly.

resolution.refute_evidence: NP-07 contract scope (Exception vs BaseException) not definitively proven from code alone. sys.exit() from a registered handler is adversarial/pathological. Downgraded to medium pending NP-07 spec review.

findings.title: _parse_tool_list() always returns [] — production tool discovery permanently broken
findings.description: mcp_adapter.py L221-227 stub unconditionally returns []. Any non-monkeypatched call to list_tools() returns []. execute() always hits 'unknown tool: <name>'.
findings.suggested_fix: Implement MCP tools/list JSON-RPC response parsing, or raise NotImplementedError to make stub state visible.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: _connect_stdio() missing stdin=PIPE — MCP server waiting for initialize hangs
findings.description: Popen at L165-170 has stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE but no stdin=PIPE. Child inherits parent stdin. MCP servers that wait for JSON-RPC 'initialize' before responding deadlock.
findings.suggested_fix: Add stdin=subprocess.PIPE to Popen call. Send MCP initialize request before reading tool list.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: Embedding/classifier lateral circuits are one-way latches — never recover
findings.description: record_embedding_failure() sets _embedding_down=True at count >= 3. No record_embedding_success() exists to reset. Embedding search stays on tsvector fallback forever after 3 transient failures.
findings.suggested_fix: Add record_embedding_success() and record_classifier_success() that decrement counts and reset _down flags.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: record_llm_success() only recovers LEVEL_1 and LEVEL_3 — other levels sticky forever
findings.description: L111-115 recovery check: only if _level == LEVEL_1 or LEVEL_3. LEVEL_2/4/5 never recover regardless of consecutive successes. Violates FR-99.
findings.suggested_fix: Change condition to if self._level != self.LEVEL_0, implement full stepped recovery ladder.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: Both-judges-failed fallback emits score=0 — violates 1-5 scale (FR-65)
findings.description: L324 JudgeResult(politeness=0, accuracy=0). SRS FR-65 scale is 1-5. 0 passed to aggregate_csat() produces CSAT below contractual floor with no error signal.
findings.suggested_fix: Use politeness=1, accuracy=1 as minimum valid scores, or add is_degraded: bool field.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: evaluate() registry import outside NP-07 protection — ImportError bypasses fault tolerance
findings.description: L228-229 deferred import and get_service('dummy') execute before asyncio.gather() and all _invoke_safely guards. Any import failure propagates as unhandled exception from evaluate().
findings.suggested_fix: Remove dead get_service('dummy') call, or wrap in try/except Exception: pass.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: Rate-limit check only tests status==429, ignores allowed=False — bypass possible
findings.description: chain.py L159 only checks getattr(rate_outcome, 'status', 200) == 429. All other stages use _is_allowed(). Rate-limiter returning allowed=False, status=200 is silently passed through.
findings.suggested_fix: Change to: if not _is_allowed(rate_outcome, default=False) or getattr(rate_outcome, 'status', 200) == 429
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: ip_outcome.status/.body bare attribute access — AttributeError crashes request
findings.description: chain.py L141-143 does bare ip_outcome.status and ip_outcome.body after defensive _is_allowed() check. Non-conforming ip_whitelist.is_allowed() return (plain bool, minimal object) triggers AttributeError.
findings.suggested_fix: Use getattr(ip_outcome, 'status', 403) and getattr(ip_outcome, 'body', b''). Mirror TLS block at L129-131.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: MessengerWebhookVerifier missing sha256= prefix guard — bare hex accepted
findings.description: verifiers.py L124-130 uses removeprefix('sha256=') which is no-op if prefix absent. WhatsAppWebhookVerifier correctly checks startswith. Inconsistency allows attacker to send bare hex digest without prefix.
findings.suggested_fix: Add: if not received_signature.startswith('sha256='): return False
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: _await_coro_from_sync thread not cancelled on join timeout — resource leak and shared state race
findings.description: L636-643 join(timeout) fires TimeoutError but daemon thread continues running. Network connection leaked. Coroutine eventual completion may mutate shared state (cache, rate-limit counter) racing with next request.
findings.suggested_fix: After timeout: call new_loop.call_soon_threadsafe(new_loop.stop) to halt the daemon loop.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

findings.title: process_embedding_job never increments retry_count — infinite retry loop possible
findings.description: L267-274 retrying branch does not mutate job.retry_count. Re-enqueueing same object keeps retries_left=True forever; max_retries is never reached.
findings.suggested_fix: Add job.retry_count += 1 before return in retrying branch.
findings.verify_evidence: Not adversarially verified.

resolution_summary.resolver_note: 11 confirmed critical+high findings verified resolved via on-disk source code inspection at .methodology/bug_hunt_report.json update time. See resolution.fix_commit and resolution.repro_test per finding.
